proposals.statement: There is no classic short-seller report (e.g. Hindenburg/Muddy Waters) driving this situation; the analogue shareholder-pressure dynamic was a contested takeover and proxy/tender-offer solicitation fight. After the WBD board agreed to sell to Netflix, Paramount launched a hostile takeover bid for WBD, taking an all-cash offer directly to shareholders.

source.title: Paramount launches a hostile takeover bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (CNN Business)

proposals.quote: Paramount launches a hostile takeover bid for Warner Bros.
proposals.locator: CNN Business, headline and lede, Dec 8 2025

proposals.statement: Paramount publicly urged WBD shareholders to side with its bid against the Netflix deal, stating it would solicit proxies against approval of the Netflix transaction at WBD's special meeting.

source.title: Paramount Enhances Its Superior $30 Per Share All-Cash Offer for Warner Bros. Discovery (Paramount press release)

proposals.quote: Paramount will solicit proxies against approval of the Netflix transaction at WBD's upcoming special meeting.
proposals.locator: Paramount press release, regulatory-progress section (re: special meeting)

proposals.statement: Paramount explicitly urged WBD shareholders to register their preference for Paramount's offer with the WBD board by tendering their shares, escalating the contested solicitation directly to the shareholder base.

source.title: Paramount Enhances Its Superior $30 Per Share All-Cash Offer for Warner Bros. Discovery (Paramount press release)

proposals.quote: PARAMOUNT URGES WBD SHAREHOLDERS TO REGISTER THEIR PREFERENCE FOR PARAMOUNT'S SUPERIOR OFFER WITH THE WBD BOARD OF DIRECTORS BY TENDERING THEIR SHARES TODAY.

proposals.statement: The WBD board pushed back on the hostile bid: it unanimously reaffirmed its recommendation in favor of the Netflix combination and recommended that WBD shareholders reject Paramount's (PSKY's) tender offer.

source.title: Warner Bros. Discovery Board of Directors Unanimously Recommends Shareholders Reject Amended Paramount Tender Offer (PR Newswire)

proposals.quote: The Board unanimously reiterates its recommendation in support of the Netflix combination and recommends that WBD shareholders reject PSKY's offer.
proposals.locator: PR Newswire WBD board release, board recommendation paragraph

proposals.statement: WBD's board characterized Paramount's offer as 'inferior' to the Netflix merger across multiple key areas, per a statement attributed to a WBD board representative.

source.title: Warner Bros. Discovery Board of Directors Unanimously Recommends Shareholders Reject Amended Paramount Tender Offer (PR Newswire)

proposals.quote: The Board unanimously determined that the Paramount's latest offer remains inferior to our merger agreement with Netflix across multiple key areas,
proposals.locator: PR Newswire WBD board release, board chairman quote

proposals.statement: WBD claimed that more than 93% of its shareholders had rejected Paramount's bid (which WBD labeled an 'inferior scheme') in favor of the Netflix sale, during the tender-offer contest.

source.title: Warner Bros. Discovery Says Over 93% of Shareholders Have 'Rejected Paramount's Inferior Scheme' in Favor of Netflix Deal (Variety)

proposals.quote: It’s also clear our shareholders agree, with more than 93% also rejecting Paramount’s inferior scheme.
proposals.locator: Variety, WBD statement quoted in body

proposals.statement: The contest escalated until the WBD board reversed course: it determined that Paramount's revised proposal, valuing WBD at $31.00 per share, constituted a 'Company Superior Proposal', giving Netflix a four-business-day match period.

source.title: Warner Bros. Discovery Board of Directors Determines Revised Proposal from Paramount Skydance Constitutes a 'Company Superior Proposal' (WBD)

proposals.quote: Discovery Board of Directors Determines Revised Proposal from Paramount Skydance Constitutes a “Company Superior Proposal” Revised PSKY Proposal Values WBD at Per Share Price of $31.00; Netflix Now Has a Four Business Day Match Period
proposals.locator: WBD news release, headline and subhead

proposals.statement: The central bear thesis on the deal is leverage/structure: WBD's board argued Paramount (PSKY) had a roughly $14 billion market capitalization while attempting an acquisition requiring $94.65 billion of debt and equity financing — nearly seven times its total market capitalization. (Interested-party characterization by the target board opposing the bid.)

source.title: Warner Bros. Discovery Board of Directors Unanimously Recommends Shareholders Reject Amended Paramount Tender Offer (PR Newswire)

proposals.quote: PSKY is a company with a $14 billion market capitalization attempting an acquisition requiring $94.65 billion of debt and equity financing, nearly seven times its total market capitalization.
proposals.locator: PR Newswire WBD board release, 'Lack of Certainty in PSKY's Ability to Close' section

proposals.statement: WBD's board framed the transaction as 'the largest LBO in history' with $87 billion of total pro forma gross debt and estimated gross leverage of approximately 7x 2026E EBITDA before synergies — the core value-destruction/leverage argument against the deal. (Interested-party characterization by the opposing target board.)

source.title: Warner Bros. Discovery Board of Directors Unanimously Recommends Shareholders Reject Amended Paramount Tender Offer (PR Newswire)

proposals.quote: In fact, it would be the largest LBO in history with $87 billion of total pro forma gross debt and an estimated gross leverage of approximately 7x 2026E EBITDA before synergies.
proposals.locator: PR Newswire WBD board release, 'Lack of Certainty in PSKY's Ability to Close' section

proposals.statement: WBD's board argued Paramount intended to incur an extraordinary amount of incremental debt — more than $50 billion — through arrangements with multiple financing partners, reinforcing the bear case that the deal loads the combined entity with debt. (Interested-party characterization.)

source.title: Warner Bros. Discovery Board of Directors Unanimously Recommends Shareholders Reject Amended Paramount Tender Offer (PR Newswire)

proposals.quote: To effect the transaction, it intends to incur an extraordinary amount of incremental debt – more than $50 billion – through arrangements with multiple financing partners.
proposals.locator: PR Newswire WBD board release, 'Lack of Certainty in PSKY's Ability to Close' section

proposals.statement: A bear/market read of Netflix's withdrawal: when Netflix dropped out of the bidding war, it stated 'The deal is no longer financially attractive, so we are declining to match the Paramount Skydance bid' — a competing bidder publicly signaling that the $31 price had exceeded what it considered financially attractive, which analysts read as implying Paramount may be overpaying.

source.title: Netflix shockingly bows out, leaving Paramount as the apparent Warner Bros. winner (CNN Business)

proposals.quote: “The deal is no longer financially attractive, so we are declining to match the Paramount Skydance bid,” the streamer said, suddenly ending the corporate tug-of-war.
proposals.locator: CNN Business, Feb 26 2026, paragraph on Netflix's withdrawal statement

proposals.statement: Even as it pursued the whole company, Paramount cast doubt on the value of WBD's cable/linear assets — an internal tension in the bull case that supports the bear read that Paramount is absorbing a declining asset to win the studio/streaming crown jewels.

source.title: Netflix shockingly bows out, leaving Paramount as the apparent Warner Bros. winner (CNN Business)

proposals.quote: Paramount has repeatedly said that it was seeking to acquire all of WBD, including the cable assets, even though it cast doubt on the actual value of those assets.
proposals.locator: CNN Business, Feb 26 2026, paragraph on Paramount's pursuit of cable assets

proposals.statement: The shareholder-pressure dynamic here is a two-sided contested-solicitation / hostile-tender fight, not an activist short campaign: Paramount solicited WBD shareholders against the Netflix deal and urged them to tender (act_2, act_3) after going hostile (act_1), while the WBD board solicited shareholders to reject Paramount's 'inferior' bid (act_4, act_5, act_6) — a contest that resolved only when the board accepted Paramount's escalated $31.00 offer as superior (act_7).

The most developed bear thesis on this deal is a leverage/financeability case, advanced by the WBD board while it opposed the bid: Paramount (~$14B market cap) is funding a ~$94.65B financing — nearly 7x its own market cap — that would be 'the largest LBO in history' at ~$87B pro forma gross debt and ~7x 2026E EBITDA before synergies (act_8, act_9, act_10). Because the proponent was an interested party opposing the offer, this thesis is corroborated as directionally consistent by Netflix — a competing bidder — walking away when it judged $31 'no longer financially attractive' (act_11) and by Paramount itself doubting the value of the cable assets it is buying (act_12).
